content: Correct order for reaction with alcoholic KOH

Hence, correct order is A > C > B > D.

content: Correct order of electrophillic substitution reaction is
explanation:
Electron-donating groups (EDGs) bonded to a benzene ring activate the ring by the $+I$ effect, enhancing the electrophilic substitution reaction. Conversely, electron-withdrawing groups (EWGs) show a $-I$ effect, reducing the ring's tendency for electrophilic substitution.

Alkyl vs. Alkoxy Groups :

An alkyl group such as $ -\mathrm{CH_3} $ exhibits a $+I$ effect but less so than a methoxy group ($-\mathrm{OCH_3}$) due to the presence of the oxygen atom. Therefore, $\mathrm{C_6H_5OCH_3}$ shows better electrophilic substitution than $\mathrm{C_6H_5CH_3}$.

Chlorine vs. Formyl Groups :

Chlorine ($\mathrm{Cl}$) exhibits both $-I$ and $+R$ effects due to its lone pair of electrons, while the formyl group ($-\mathrm{CHO}$) has a $-R$ nature. Consequently, $\mathrm{C_6H_5Cl}$ shows more electrophilic substitution than $\mathrm{C_6H_5CHO}$.

Groups exhibiting a $+I$ and $+R$ effect show a higher tendency for electrophilic substitution than those showing either or $-I$ and $+R$ effects.

Therefore, the correct order is :

$ B > A > C > D $

and option (c) is the correct choice.

content: Which of the following represents Wurtz-Fittig reaction?
explanation:
The Wurtz-Fittig reaction is the reaction of an aryl halide with alkyl halide and sodium metal to give substituted aromatic compound.

Thus, Wurtz Fittig reaction is

$$\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{I}+2 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{I} \longrightarrow \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{CH}_3+2 \mathrm{NaI}$$

content: A bromoalkane '$$X$$' reacts with magnesium in dry ether to form compound '$$Y$$'. The reaction of '$$Y$$' with methanol followed by hydrolysis yield an alcohol having molecular formula, $$\mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}$$. The compound '$$X$$' is

Assertion (A) The boiling points of alkyl halides decrease in the order $$R \mathrm{I}>R \mathrm{Br}>\mathrm{RCl}>\mathrm{RF}$$.

Reason (R) The boiling points of alkyl chlorides, bromides and iodides are considerably higher than that of the hydrocarbon of comparable molecular mass.

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation:
Assertion and reason both are correct statements but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion. For the same hydrocarbon part, boiling point depends upon the atomic mass of halogen atom. Higher the mass of the halogen atom, higher will be the boiling point. So, we can say that boiling point decreases with decrease in atomic mass of halogen atom. Thus, the decreasing order of boiling point is

$$R \mathrm{I}>R \mathrm{Br}>R \mathrm{Cl}>R \mathrm{~F}$$
